name: Pellorneidae
description: Pellorneidae, the ground babblers, are a family of passerine birds found in tropical Asia and Africa. They are primarily terrestrial birds that forage on the forest floor, feeding on insects and other invertebrates.

children.name: Pellorneum
children.description: A genus of terrestrial babblers with streaked or spotted underparts, found in Asian forests.

children.name: Pellorneum ruficeps
children.description: A brownish babbler with heavily streaked white underparts and a rufous crown, skulking on the forest floor across South and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Pellorneum malaccense
children.description: A stocky, short-tailed babbler with plain brown upperparts and whitish underparts with a buff wash on the breast.

children.name: Pellorneum rostratum
children.description: A babbler with a distinctive white throat and breast contrasting with brown upperparts, found in lowland forests and swampy areas of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo.

children.name: Pellorneum tickelli
children.description: A warm brown babbler with a buff-coloured breast and faintly streaked throat, found in hill forests from the eastern Himalayas through mainland Southeast Asia.

children.name: Pellorneum pyrrogenys
children.description: A babbler with a rufous crown, olive-brown upperparts, and whitish underparts with heavy dark streaking, found in lowland and hill forests of Java, Borneo, and Sumatra.

children.name: Pellorneum bicolor
children.description: A striking babbler with rich rufous-brown upperparts, a grey head, and white underparts, inhabiting lowland and foothill forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo.

children.name: Pellorneum palustre
children.description: A babbler of wetlands and reedbeds in north-eastern India and Bangladesh, classified as Vulnerable due to habitat loss.

children.name: Pellorneum nigrocapitatum
children.description: A babbler of the Malay Peninsula with a black cap, olive-brown upperparts, and white underparts with heavy dark streaking.

children.name: Pellorneum cinereiceps
children.description: A babbler endemic to Palawan in the Philippines, with a grey head, olive-brown back, and pale underparts.

children.name: Pellorneum celebense
children.description: A plain brown babbler endemic to Sulawesi and surrounding islands.

children.name: Pellorneum albiventre
children.description: A small babbler with a spotted white throat, found from the eastern Himalayas to southern China and mainland Southeast Asia.

children.name: Pellorneum capistratum
children.description: A babbler of Java and Bali with a black crown, white throat, and olive-brown upperparts. Its song is a series of clear, descending whistles.

children.name: Pellorneum capistratoides
children.description: A babbler of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo, with a black cap and white underparts. Recently split from the Javan Black-capped Babbler.

children.name: Pellorneum spiculifolium
children.description: This small ground babbler is found in the arid regions of sub-Saharan Africa, known for its distinctive calls and behavior that help it blend into its desert environment.

children.name: Malacocincla corynoides
children.description: This ground babbler species is found in the warm, dry regions of southern Asia and Australia, where it forages on leaf litter and seeds.

children.name: Napothera fulviventris
children.description: This species is a vibrant orange color with white markings, found in the dense rainforests of South American cloud forests. It's known for its melodic calls that help it communicate through dense vegetation.

children.name: Malacopteron fulvum
children.description: This species has a distinctive orange and black coloration, with fluffy feathers on its head and neck. It is found in the high-altitude grasslands of South America.

children.name: Illadopsis fulvus
children.description: This small ground babbler species is characterized by its bright orange coloration, which helps it stand out against the surrounding foliage. It's found in the dense rainforests of South America.

children.name: Malacocincla notabilis
children.description: Endemic to the western parts of China, this species is known for its distinctive vocalizations and habitat preferences.

children.name: Napothera insignis
children.description: Found in the dense forests of Southeast Asia, this species is recognized by its bold plumage and shy nature.

children.name: Malacopteron melanocephala
children.description: A rare species, found in the mountainous regions of Vietnam and China, known for its striking black head and brown body.

children.name: Kenopia aurea
children.description: This species is known for its golden-yellow plumage and melodic songs, found in the open forests of India.

children.name: Turdinus altipennis
children.description: Endemic to the high-altitude regions of Nepal and Bhutan, this species is well-adapted to cold climates.

children.name: Gypsophila rubescens
children.description: This species is known for its red-tinted feathers and prefers scrublands in parts of India.

children.name: Laticilla gracilis
children.description: Found in the grasslands of central China, this species is characterized by its slender build and unique plumage patterns.

children.name: Illadopsis alpina
children.description: This species is found in the mountainous regions of Tibet and Nepal, known for its adaptability to harsh environments.

children.name: Ptyrticus cineraceus
children.description: Known for its ashy plumage and preference for forest edges, this species is widespread in the Eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Graminicola fuliginosa
children.description: Found in the grasslands of northern India, this species is known for its rich brown coloration and ground-dwelling habits.

children.name: Pellorneum pulchellus
children.description: This species is known for its vibrant plumage and melodious songs, found in the deciduous forests of southern China.

children.name: Malacocincla
children.description: A genus of medium-sized, plain-coloured ground babblers found in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Malacocincla abbotti
children.description: A plain brown babbler with a whitish throat and short tail, skulking in dense undergrowth across Southeast Asia.

children.name: Malacocincla sepiaria
children.description: A brown babbler found in lowland and foothill forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Java, and Borneo.

children.name: Malacocincla perspicillata
children.description: A mysterious babbler of Borneo, known from a single specimen collected in the 1840s and rediscovered in 2020. It has a prominent black brow and a grey throat.

children.name: Malacocincla cinereiceps
children.description: A babbler with an ashy-grey head, olive-brown back, and whitish underparts, found in the montane forests of Borneo.

children.name: Napothera
children.description: A genus of small, short-tailed babblers that inhabit dense forest undergrowth in Asia.

children.name: Napothera epilepidota
children.description: A tiny babbler with dark scaled and streaked plumage, found in dense understory from the Himalayas to Sumatra.

children.name: Napothera crispifrons
children.description: A dark, robust wren-babbler of limestone karst habitats in mainland Southeast Asia.

children.name: Napothera crassa
children.description: A uniform chocolate-brown wren-babbler of montane forests in Sumatra, Borneo, and the Malay Peninsula.

children.name: Napothera brunnescens
children.description: A recently split wren-babbler from Java, formerly considered conspecific with N. crassa.

children.name: Napothera pasquieri
children.description: A rare wren-babbler endemic to central Vietnam and adjacent Laos, classified as Vulnerable.

children.name: Napothera danjoui
children.description: A wren-babbler of limestone karst in Laos and Vietnam, with a yellow belly and heavily streaked upperparts.

children.name: Napothera rabori
children.description: A wren-babbler endemic to Luzon in the Philippines, named after the Filipino ornithologist Dioscoro Rabor. It is classified as Vulnerable.

children.name: Malacopteron
children.description: A genus of medium-sized babblers with rufous or chestnut crowns, found in Southeast Asian forests.

children.name: Malacopteron cinereum
children.description: A babbler with a chestnut crown with scaly markings, foraging in the mid-story of lowland rainforests across Southeast Asia.

children.name: Malacopteron magnum
children.description: A large babbler with a rich rufous crown, found in lowland forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo.

children.name: Malacopteron affine
children.description: A babbler with a sooty-grey crown, found in lowland forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo.

children.name: Malacopteron albogulare
children.description: A small babbler with a grey breast and white throat, found in lowland forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo.

children.name: Malacopteron magnirostre
children.description: A babbler of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo, with a distinctive black moustachial stripe and a stout bill.

children.name: Kenopia
children.description: A monotypic genus of wren-babbler with striking black-and-white striped plumage, found in Southeast Asian forests.

children.name: Kenopia striata
children.description: A striking babbler with bold black-and-white stripes on the head and upperparts.

children.name: Turdinus
children.description: A genus of large wren-babblers with stout bills and thrush-like proportions, found in Southeast Asian forests.

children.name: Turdinus macrodactylus
children.description: A large, dark babbler of montane forests in the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Java.

children.name: Turdinus atrigularis
children.description: A babbler with a distinctive black throat, endemic to the montane forests of Borneo.

children.name: Turdinus marmoratus
children.description: A cryptically patterned wren-babbler with mottled brown and black upperparts.

children.name: Turdinus everetti
children.description: A wren-babbler endemic to the mountains of Borneo, with a rich chestnut back.

children.name: Turdinus rufipectus
children.description: A wren-babbler with a rusty-red breast, found in montane forests of Sumatra.

children.name: Turdinus brevicaudatus
children.description: A wren-babbler of the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia, with heavily streaked upperparts and a short tail.

children.name: Gypsophila
children.description: A genus of wren-babblers adapted to limestone karst habitats in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Gypsophila crassa
children.description: A large, dark wren-babbler of montane forests in Sumatra, Borneo, and the Malay Peninsula.

children.name: Gypsophila crispifrons
children.description: A wren-babbler of limestone karst in Thailand, Myanmar, and the Malay Peninsula.

children.name: Gypsophila annamensis
children.description: A recently described wren-babbler of limestone karst in central Vietnam and Laos.

children.name: Gypsophila calcicola
children.description: A wren-babbler of limestone karst in Myanmar and Thailand, recently split from G. crispifrons.

children.name: Laticilla
children.description: A genus of long-tailed, grassland-dwelling babblers found in South Asia.

children.name: Laticilla burnesii
children.description: A small, long-tailed babbler of tall grasslands and reed beds in Pakistan and northwestern India.

children.name: Laticilla cinerascens
children.description: A dull brown babbler of swamp grasslands in the Brahmaputra floodplains of India and Bangladesh.

children.name: Illadopsis
children.description: A genus of African forest babblers, the only pellorneid genus found in Africa. They are secretive, thrush-like birds of the forest understorey.

children.name: Illadopsis fulvescens
children.description: A brown babbler with a pale throat and breast, found in lowland forests of West and Central Africa.

children.name: Illadopsis rufipennis
children.description: A small babbler with a pale grey breast and rufous wings, found in forests across West and Central Africa.

children.name: Illadopsis puveli
children.description: A babbler of West African forests, with olive-brown upperparts and white underparts. Named after a French collector.

children.name: Illadopsis albipectus
children.description: A babbler of Central African forests, with a distinctive scaly pattern on the white breast.

children.name: Illadopsis cleaveri
children.description: A babbler with a black cap, grey breast, and olive-brown back, found in West and Central African forests.

children.name: Illadopsis pyrrhoptera
children.description: A babbler of East African montane forests, with a rufous cap, grey breast, and brown back.

children.name: Illadopsis rufescens
children.description: A rufous-brown babbler of West African lowland forests, with a pale throat and belly.

children.name: Illadopsis distans
children.description: A recently split babbler from the Eastern Arc Mountains of Tanzania, separated from the Mountain Illadopsis on genetic and vocal grounds.

children.name: Illadopsis turdina
children.description: A large, thrush-like babbler of West African forests, with spotted underparts and a stout bill.

children.name: Ptyrticus
children.description: A monotypic African genus of thrush-like babbler, found in the miombo woodlands of south-central Africa.

children.name: Ptyrticus turdinus
children.description: A large, thrush-like babbler of miombo woodlands in Central and East Africa, with brown upperparts and heavily spotted white underparts.

children.name: Graminicola
children.description: A genus of two species of long-tailed, grass-dwelling babblers found in South and Southeast Asian grasslands.

children.name: Graminicola bengalensis
children.description: A long-tailed, streaked babbler of tall grasslands in South Asia, with a distinctive rufous rump. Classified as Near Threatened.

children.name: Graminicola striatus
children.description: A long-tailed, heavily streaked babbler of grasslands in southern China and mainland Southeast Asia.
